stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around a major LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and performance tracking for a specific Meta-derived LLM variant.

stories.title: Hackers likely hijacked over 20,000 Instagram accounts with Meta’s AI chatbot
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Hackers compromised over 20,000 Instagram accounts via Meta's AI support chatbot.
The exploit allowed account hijacking without requiring two-factor authentication.
Meta attributed the vulnerability to a software 'bug' in the chatbot system.

stories.title: Uber tells London to get ready for robotaxis
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Uber is planning to deploy autonomous robotaxis in London.
The service, utilizing Wayve's autonomous vehicles, is slated for a rollout later this year.
Uber is currently collecting an interest list from potential early riders.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking easy access to passive investment capital for SpaceX.
The exclusion extends to major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This signals a potential shift in how large, high-growth, or speculative tech companies are integrated into mainstream investment indices.

stories.title: How AI Agents Reshape Knowledge Work: Autonomy, Efficiency, and Scope
stories.author: Jerry Ma
bullet_points: The shift in frontier AI is moving from simple conversational assistants to autonomous agents capable of end-to-end task execution.
The research uses production data from Perplexity's Search and Computer products to empirically study this transition.
The study yields three key findings regarding how AI agents accelerate and reshape knowledge work.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the DeepSeek_v3 family and is suitable for conversational tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now integrates MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework.
This integration aims to provide the fastest possible performance for running Ollama on Apple Silicon.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
This emphasizes a trend toward private, on-device AI capabilities rather than cloud-dependent solutions.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.
The tool supports running various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon
stories.author: Cyrus Farivar
bullet_points: A survivor of a school shooting is suing an AI gun detection firm due to system failure.
The case raises critical questions regarding the required accuracy and reliability of AI in life-critical applications.
This highlights the real-world, high-stakes implications and potential liability of current AI surveillance technology.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: MemDreamer addresses the token explosion and attention dilution issues in long video understanding for Vision-Language Models.
It decouples perception and reasoning by framing long-video understanding as an agentic exploration process.
The framework utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory with a three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction.

stories.title: Act As a Real Researcher: A Suite of Benchmarks Evaluating Frontier LLMs and Agentic Harnesses in Research Lifecycle
stories.author: Xiangyong Cao
bullet_points: New benchmarks evaluate frontier LLMs and agentic harnesses across the entire research lifecycle.
Agents show proficiency in complex coding and autonomous experiment execution, marking a shift from assistants to agents.
Current limitations in field sensitivity, ethics, and scientific judgment prevent agents from fully replacing human researchers.

stories.title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
stories.author: Chunhua Shen
bullet_points: MemDreamer addresses the token explosion and attention dilution issues in long-video understanding for Vision-Language Models.
It decouples perception and reasoning by framing long-video analysis as an agentic exploration process.
The framework utilizes a Hierarchical Graph Memory with a three-tier architecture for semantic abstraction.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, leveraging local LLM capabilities.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run Claude Code-like functionality with open-source models via Ollama.
It bridges the gap between proprietary models (Anthropic) and the open-source ecosystem (Ollama).

stories.title: Microsoft’s AI chief says superintelligence is near, but won’t take your job
stories.author: Nilay Patel
bullet_points: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman discussed the proximity of superintelligence.
Suleyman addressed concerns about AI replacing human jobs.
The interview covered his approach to training new AI models.

stories.title: NASA will wear high-tech Prada long johns to the Moon
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: NASA is collaborating with Prada on the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG) for Artemis IV.
The LCVG serves as the essential base layer worn underneath the Axiom Extravehicular Mobility Unit (AxEMU) spacesuit.
This technology is slated for use during the Artemis IV mission, scheduled for 2028.

stories.title: The weather and climate science AI revolution isn’t revolutionary
stories.author: Scott K. Johnson
bullet_points: The article critiques the hype surrounding AI in weather and climate science.
It argues that current applications of machine learning have inherent limitations.
The piece prompts a deeper discussion on the practical, rather than purely theoretical, use of ML.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker has been shown to potentially infect a PC without physical user interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The seller of the device does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares has successfully achieved criticality with a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in a testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This development is relevant to the growing field of advanced nuclear energy technology.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs on discrete probability problems to test probabilistic reasoning.
Models show high accuracy (0.96) on standard probability exercises.
Performance drops significantly (0.59) when tested on counterintuitive problems, highlighting reasoning limitations.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, a major challenge in AI.
Proposes SETA (Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning) to mitigate catastrophic forgetting.
The framework distinguishes between task-specific knowledge and shared capabilities using adaptive sparse experts.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: HPC clusters are shifting from traditional deterministic pipelines to new AI-driven paradigms.
AI integration introduces iterative, data-driven, and probabilistic characteristics to scientific workflows.
The transition presents unique computational challenges for traditional high-performance computing environments.

stories.title: Whisper Hallucination Detection and Mitigation via Hidden Representation Steering and Sparse AutoEncoders
stories.author: Assel Yermekova
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'hallucinations' in the widely used Whisper ASR model.
Proposes using internal representations (raw activations and Sparse AutoEncoders) for detection and mitigation.
Demonstrates that these representation spaces successfully encode linearly separable hallucination-related information.

stories.title: Planning-aligned Token Compression for Long-Context Autonomous Driving
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of monolithic vision-action models in autonomous driving when handling long temporal contexts.
Proposes 'Planning-aligned Token Compression' as a lightweight solution compatible with existing architectures without requiring backbone modifications.
Identifies the limitations of current compression methods, which are often rule-based, suggesting a need for more advanced techniques.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, a major challenge in AI.
Proposes SETA (Mixture of Sparse Experts for Task Agnostic Continual Learning) to improve knowledge retention.
The framework distinguishes between task-specific knowledge and shared capabilities using adaptive sparse experts.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: HPC clusters are shifting from traditional deterministic pipelines to AI-driven workflows.
AI integration introduces new computational paradigms characterized by iteration, data-driven nature, and probabilistic outcomes.
The transition presents unique technical challenges related to managing these new, non-linear workflows.

stories.title: CoMetaPNS: Continually Meta-learning Personalized Neural Surrogates for Cardiac Electrophysiology Simulations
stories.author: Linwei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces CoMetaPNS, a method for continually meta-learning personalized neural surrogates.
Addresses the dual challenges of model personalization and high computational cost in cardiac electrophysiology simulations.
Utilizes few-shot generative modeling and set-conditioned surrogates to learn the personalization process with limited data.

stories.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
stories.author: Bernardo Busoni
bullet_points: The study benchmarks LLMs' probabilistic reasoning using controlled dice-rolling problems.
Models perform highly on standard probability exercises (0.96 accuracy) but struggle with counterintuitive ones (0.59 accuracy).
The research evaluates 8 state-of-the-art models, including the impact of Chain-of-Thought prompting.

stories.title: Agentopia: Long-Term Life Simulation and Learning in Agent Societies
stories.author: Yunzhe Tao
bullet_points: The research explores using LLM-powered agents to simulate long-term life and social interactions.
It addresses a limitation in prior agent society simulations, which were typically too short (days) to capture deep social growth.
The goal is to determine if LLMs can learn complex human behaviors by experiencing simulated social life.

stories.title: Your UnEmbedding Matrix is Secretly a Feature Lens for Text Embeddings
stories.author: Rui Yan
bullet_points: LLMs show strong zero-shot capabilities but perform poorly as standalone embedding models.
The research suggests text embeddings align with frequent but uninformative tokens in the vocabulary space.
The paper proposes that the 'UnEmbedding Matrix' acts as a feature lens causing suboptimal embedding performance.

stories.title: TEVI: Text-Conditioned Editing of Visual Representations via Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Vision-Language Alignment
stories.author: Bernt Schiele
bullet_points: Introduces TEVI, a framework designed to improve vision-language alignment in models like CLIP.
Addresses the issue of information imbalance where image embeddings contain more information than their text captions describe.
TEVI uses captions as a selective signal to guide the editing and refinement of image embeddings.

stories.title: Sycophantic Praise: Evaluating Excessive Praise in Language Models
stories.author: Tianyu Jiang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the under-researched area of explicit praise and flattery in Language Models (LLMs), distinguishing it from standard sycophancy (excessive agreement).
It proposes a novel, parameterized framework to quantify whether praise is excessive relative to the actual quality of the contribution and the user's expected ability.
The framework demonstrates superior performance compared to existing generic LLM judgment methods for measuring this specific alignment issue.

stories.title: The Lipreading Gap: Do VSR Models Perceive Visual Speech Like Human Lipreaders?
stories.author: Naomi Harte
bullet_points: VSR models have surpassed human lipreaders on standard benchmarks, but the study questions the nature of this success.
The research compares three VSR systems against human baselines using word, character, phoneme, and viseme-level metrics.
Despite higher overall accuracy, models exhibit different strengths and weaknesses compared to human lipreading abilities.

stories.title: The Masked Advantage: Uncovering Local-Language Access to Cultural Knowledge in LLMs
stories.author: Michalis Vazirgiannis
bullet_points: Investigates whether local cultural knowledge in LLMs is better accessed via English or the local language.
Identifies limitations in current evaluations, such as reliance on rigid, template-based questions.
Proposes new methods to disentangle general language proficiency from language-conditioned knowledge access.

stories.title: M$^3$Exam: Benchmarking Multimodal Memory for Realistic User-Agent Interactions
stories.author: Xiaofang Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces M^3Exam, a new benchmark designed to test multimodal conversational memory in language agents.
Addresses limitations in current benchmarks by focusing on realistic user-agent interactions rather than simplified human-human dialogues.
Evaluates agents on complex tasks, including reasoning over authentic multimodal file interaction and interpreting concealed user information.

stories.title: Launch HN: Intuned (YC S22) – Build and run reliable browser automations as code
stories.author: fkilaiwi
bullet_points: Intuned offers a platform for building, deploying, and maintaining browser automations as code.
The core feature is an AI agent designed to automate websites lacking public APIs for tasks like scraping and form submission.
A key differentiator is the agent's ability to automatically 'heal' automations when the target website undergoes changes.

stories.title: I Built an Adversarial Eval Framework and Attacked 5 LLMs — Every Single One Failed
stories.author: saurav_bhattacharya
bullet_points: An adversarial evaluation framework was developed to test the robustness of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The framework tested several prominent models (Llama, Qwen, GPT-OSS) across 10 adversarial scenarios.
The results showed that all tested LLMs performed poorly, with none scoring above 63%.
